skill.name: cc-skill-security-review
skill.description: Security review checklist for authentication, input validation, secrets, API endpoints, sensitive data, dependencies, and deployment readiness.
skill.summary: Review application code against practical security controls before release.
skill.author: affaan-m

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many command, network, credential, storage, and obfuscation patterns, but they appear inside Markdown examples and checklist text, not executable skill code. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found. One real content issue remains: the CSP example recommends unsafe-eval and unsafe-inline, which weakens security guidance.

medium_findings.title: Unsafe CSP Guidance in Security Checklist
medium_findings.description: The Content Security Policy example includes unsafe-eval and unsafe-inline while presenting the snippet as recommended XSS prevention guidance. This can normalize weak production CSP settings for users following the checklist.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The issue is semantic, not executable behavior. The code block is clearly part of recommended security guidance, and the unsafe CSP directives are visible in that recommendation.

low_findings.title: External Command Matches Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick detections map to fenced Markdown examples and shell commands for dependency auditing. They are not runnable scripts shipped by the skill and do not create command injection by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is only SKILL.md, and the flagged content is inside Markdown code fences. No separate executable file or invocation path is present.

low_findings.title: Environment and Secret Access Matches Are Safe Examples
low_findings.description: The process.env and secret-looking strings are used to teach safe secrets management and to show hardcoded secrets as a negative example. No real credential value or exfiltration path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding headings label hardcoded secrets as forbidden and environment variables as recommended. The values are placeholders and remain inside documentation.

low_findings.title: Browser Storage and Sensitive File Matches Are Checklist Text
low_findings.description: The localStorage, cookie, .env.local, browser credential, and Windows SAM related detections are checklist examples or scanner keyword matches. They do not show actual file reads, browser data access, or credential collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content discusses safe token storage and deployment checks. I found no code that opens credential stores or reads sensitive operating system databases.

low_findings.title: Network Matches Are Local Tests or Reference Links
low_findings.description: The fetch calls target relative application paths inside test examples, and the hardcoded URLs are public documentation resources. No outbound credential transfer or hidden endpoint was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fetch calls use relative paths and test expected HTTP status codes. The external URLs are named security documentation links in a resources section.

low_findings.title: Base64 Decode Match Is Signature Verification Guidance
low_findings.description: The Buffer base64 operations appear in a Solana wallet signature verification example. This is normal decoding for cryptographic verification, not evidence of payload obfuscation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code decodes a message signature and public key before verification. The surrounding section is explicit wallet verification guidance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Dangerous Combination Heuristic Is Not Confirmed
dangerous_patterns.description: The scanner combined code execution, network, credential, and obfuscation indicators across separate Markdown examples. The skill does not contain executable logic that chains these behaviors.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The indicators are in independent educational sections. I found no script, hook, or instruction that exfiltrates credentials or evades review.

content.value_statement: Security issues often appear when teams add authentication, APIs, file uploads, or payments. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused checklist for practical application security review.
actual_capabilities: Reviews secrets handling and environment variable usage.
Checks input validation, file upload limits, and SQL injection controls.
Evaluates authentication, authorization, cookies, and session handling.
Guides XSS, CSRF, rate limiting, logging, and error handling review.
Includes deployment checks for headers, HTTPS, dependencies, CORS, and Supabase RLS.
Covers wallet and transaction verification for Solana features.
limitations: It is a checklist skill, not an automated vulnerability scanner.
It does not prove that deployed infrastructure is configured correctly.
It depends on the model receiving relevant code and context.
Some example snippets require adaptation before production use.

use_cases.title: Pre-release feature review
use_cases.description: Review a new API, upload flow, or payment feature before production deployment.

use_cases.title: Security checklist for pull requests
use_cases.description: Apply consistent security questions during review of authentication, authorization, and data handling changes.

use_cases.title: Secure implementation guidance
use_cases.description: Ask for safer patterns when adding validation, cookies, rate limits, or dependency checks.

prompt_templates.title: Run a basic security checklist
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small change
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this change with the security checklist. Focus on secrets, input validation, authentication, authorization, and error messages.

prompt_templates.title: Review an API endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this API endpoint for injection, authorization, CSRF, rate limiting, logging, and sensitive data exposure. List concrete risks and fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a deployment path
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this feature for production readiness. Include HTTPS, headers, CORS, dependencies, database policies, secrets, and rollback concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Threat model a sensitive workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of payments or wallets
prompt_templates.prompt: Threat model this sensitive workflow. Identify assets, trust boundaries, abuse cases, missing controls, test cases, and priority fixes.

output_examples.input: Review a new user profile update endpoint.
output: High priority: verify the requester can update only their own profile.
Medium priority: validate all profile fields with length and type limits.
Add tests for unauthorized updates, invalid input, and generic error responses.

output_examples.input: Check a file upload implementation before launch.
output: Confirm size, MIME type, and extension validation before storage.
Store files outside executable paths and randomize stored names.
Add rate limits and scan logs for accidental file metadata leaks.

output_examples.input: Assess a payment or wallet confirmation flow.
output: Verify user ownership before sensitive actions.
Confirm recipient, amount, balance, and replay protection.
Log only safe identifiers and never store raw secrets or private keys.

best_practices: Provide the relevant code, routes, database rules, and deployment context together.
Ask for prioritized findings with exact remediation steps and test cases.
Review sensitive changes again after applying fixes.
anti_patterns: Do not treat checklist completion as proof of security.
Do not share real secrets, tokens, private keys, or customer data in prompts.
Do not copy example snippets into production without adapting them to your stack.

faq.question: Does this skill scan code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured security review of provided context.

faq.question: Is it only for web applications?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on web applications but also includes APIs, Supabase, dependencies, and Solana wallet checks.

faq.question: Can it replace a professional security audit?
faq.answer: No. It helps find common issues early but does not replace expert testing or compliance review.

faq.question: What code should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide changed routes, data models, auth logic, configuration, tests, and deployment notes related to the feature.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What is the main marketplace warning?
faq.answer: One CSP example uses weak directives, so users should adapt security headers carefully before production use.
